title: hymenocallis pedunculata
intro: Hymenocallis pedunculata, also known as the swamp shoal bells or swamp galatella, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Hymenocallis genus and family Amaryllidaceae. It is native to the southeastern United States and is commonly found in wetland areas. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. Herbal preparations from the plant, such as extracts and infusions, have been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, sore throats, and skin irritations, showcasing its potential health benefits.
